title: Smash
description: Smash the stack on a 32-bit Linux binary with no stack canary. Redirect execution to a win function and pop a shell.
longDescription:
A deliberately vulnerable C binary is running as a network service. It reads user input into a fixed-size stack buffer without bounds checking. No stack canary. NX disabled. Perfect for learning classic stack-based buffer overflows.

Find the offset to the return address, craft a payload, and redirect execution to the hidden win() function to get a shell and read the flag.

### What you will practice
GDB debugging, pattern offset calculation with pwntools, crafting a basic ROP chain, and writing a Python exploit script.
creator: Dock & Root Labs
objectives: Identify the vulnerable buffer and compute the overflow offset
Locate the win() function address in the binary
Craft a payload that overwrites the return address
Get a shell on the remote service and read the flag
